entries.title: Violence in Australian town after arrest of man over girl's murder
entries.snippet: There has been violence outside hospital in Alice Springs, where a man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl was being treated.

entries.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
entries.snippet: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

entries.title: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of exhibition
entries.snippet: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

entries.title: Britney Spears charged in California with driving under influence
entries.snippet: The pop star is facing one misdemeanour count of driving under the influence of any alcohol and drug.

entries.title: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
entries.snippet: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

entries.title: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
entries.snippet: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

entries.title: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
entries.snippet: At least nine people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

entries.title: Trainee driver crashes bus into River Seine
entries.snippet: The driver hit a parked car and veered off the road into the river - about 12 miles south of Paris - early on Thursday.

entries.title: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

entries.title: US House votes to end record shutdown over immigration enforcement
entries.snippet: The vote provides funding for most Department of Homeland Security agencies, except for two subagencies responsible for immigration enforcement.

entries.title: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, as spat with Merz intensifies
entries.snippet: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

entries.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
entries.snippet: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

entries.title: Man sentenced to death for murder of toddlers at Ugandan nursery
entries.snippet: Christopher Okello Onyum had pleaded insanity but this was dismissed by the judge.

entries.title: 'How are we going to get back home?' Islamist group tightens blockade on Mali capital
entries.snippet: The blockade comes days after Mali's defence minister was assassinated in a series of nationwide attacks.

entries.title: US singer D4vd bought tools online to dispose of girl's body, prosecutors allege
entries.snippet: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

entries.title: The sub-Arctic town pitching itself as Canada's gateway to Europe
entries.snippet: Churchill, Manitoba, best known as the world's polar bear capital, has ambitions of making its deep-water port a year-round trade route.

entries.title: A son overlooked and a jailed tycoon: Inside Samsung's succession drama
entries.snippet: The family dynasty behind Samsung is so complicated it regularly makes headline news in South Korea.

entries.title: Indians lost $2.5bn to digital fraud in 2025 - now its central bank is fighting back
entries.snippet: The RBI is mulling measures to tackle rising cases of digital fraud in India - but will they work?

entries.title: Three takeaways from Hegseth's clash with lawmakers over Iran war
entries.snippet: The hours-long hearing was the first time the US defence secretary had faced questions under oath since the conflict began.

entries.title: In five charts - How UAE's exit could affect Opec's influence over the oil price
entries.snippet: The BBC takes a look in charts at what the UAE's departure could mean for the oil cartel and more widely.

entries.title: Trump gives the go-ahead for a major new Canada-U.S. oil pipeline
entries.snippet: More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.

entries.title: Will.i.am wants to future-proof a new generation
entries.snippet: The Black Eyed Peas co-founder turned entrepreneur is now teaching a class on "agentic AI" for Arizona State.

entries.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick
entries.snippet: President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

entries.title: U.S. House primaries in Louisiana are suspended after Voting Rights Act ruling
entries.snippet: Louisiana suspended its upcoming primaries for the U.S. House, following Wednesday's U.S. Supreme Court ruling that the state's congressional map is an "unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

entries.title: In real-world test, an AI model did better than ER doctors at diagnosing patients
entries.snippet: Researchers evaluated how well an AI model could diagnose and make decisions about patient care.

entries.title: The Onion's bid to take over Infowars moves to the Texas Supreme Court
entries.snippet: The state's highest court will now consider a deal that would allow the Onion to license the Infowars brand name and turn the show into a mockery of itself.

entries.title: Congress ends record shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security
entries.snippet: Thursday's vote in the House provides funding for DHS after a more than two-month shutdown, but does not include dollars for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Customs and Border Protection.

entries.title: Myanmar junta says Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, doubts linger
entries.snippet: The secrecy surrounding Suu Kyi's new location has alarmed her son, Kim Aris, who says he has received no authoritative confirmation of her wellbeing.

entries.title: Craig Venter, pioneering human genome decoder, dies at 79
entries.snippet: Pioneering scientist J. Craig Venter has died at 79. His "whole genome shotgun method" helped genome sequencing become faster and cheaper.

entries.title: 'Fool me once…' Lawyers argue Kennedy Center should not meet same fate as the East Wing
entries.snippet: Lawyers made arguments in hearings for two separate lawsuits against President Trump and the Kennedy Center's board this week. Both lawsuits want to halt plans to close the performing arts venue for two years for renovations.

entries.title: What to know after House passes Homeland Security funding and ends historic shutdown
entries.snippet: The longest shutdown of a U.S. government agency in history has ended, after Trump signed a House-passed bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. The administration had warned that money to pay thousands of federal security workers would run out after Thursday wi

entries.title: Oil prices surge again as Trump weighs options to end Iran war and Hegseth faces lawmakers
entries.snippet: On Thursday, the top U.S. commander in the Middle East briefed Trump on potential military options and the ongoing blockade of the Persian Gulf while talks to end the war with Iran remain at a standstill. Meanwhile, Defense Secretary Hegseth faced more grilling from lawmakers as

entries.title: News Wrap: Gov. Janet Mills drops out of U.S. Senate race in Maine
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Thursday, Maine Gov. Mills dropped her Senate bid, Camp Mystic in Texas dropped its summer reopening plans, the Secret Service provided new details about the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, King Charles and Queen Camilla wrapped up their state visit

entries.title: Rising fuel costs put budget airlines under pressure as Spirit faces bankruptcy
entries.snippet: As the war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring, several U.S. budget airlines are asking the federal government for a $2.5 billion lifeline. The appeal comes as Spirit Airlines faces potential bankruptcy and is negotiating separately for a $500 million government bailout. Reut

entries.title: Germany builds up its military to prepare for a potential future without U.S. support
entries.snippet: Despite Trump's repeated threats to withdraw the United States from NATO, Germany's top military officer claims relations with American military leaders are as strong as ever, but he also says that the threat from Russia means Germany's push to strengthen its own armed forces is

entries.title: Fired immigration judge gives inside look at Trump's deportation agenda
entries.snippet: Since returning to office, Trump has made sweeping changes to the legal immigration system, including speeding up deportations and tamping down on asylum seekers. The Justice Department has also fired more than 100 sitting immigration judges and is now advertising to hire so-call

entries.title: How new SNAP restrictions on sugary foods and drinks are affecting Texas residents
entries.snippet: This year, 22 states are changing what people who rely on SNAP for food assistance can buy with their benefits. The new rules are meant to cut out sugary foods and drinks, a central focus of the "Make America Healthy Again" movement. But advocates fear they could cut down on what

entries.title: Trump signs Homeland Security funding bill, ending record shutdown
entries.snippet: DHS has been without routine funds since Feb. 14, causing hardship for workers, though much of Trump's immigration agenda that is central to the dispute is being funded separately.

entries.title: Gold medal skier Lindsey Vonn opens up about her devastating crash and recovery
entries.snippet: When she retired in 2019, Lindsey Vonn was already considered one of the greatest U.S. skiers of all time. In 2024, at the age of 40, Vonn returned to the slopes and went on to become the oldest World Cup winner in history. In February, the world watched as her Olympic run in Ita

entries.title: 1st direct U.S.-Venezuela commercial flight in 7 years lands in Caracas
entries.snippet: The resumption of a nonstop commercial flight between the two countries comes months after the U.S. capture of then President Nicolás Maduro in a stunning nighttime raid on his residence in Caracas in early January.

entries.title: Senate bans members and staff from betting in prediction markets
entries.snippet: Prediction markets, including the betting platform Polymarket and its chief rival Kalshi, have come under scrutiny as the business has expanded.

entries.title: Britney Spears charged with driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs
entries.snippet: Spears, who has since entered substance abuse treatment, was arrested March 4 after she was pulled over for driving her black BMW fast and erratically on U.S. 101 near her home, the California Highway Patrol said.

entries.title: Weekly U.S. jobless claims fall to 189,000, lowest in more than five decades
entries.snippet: The number of Americans filing for unemployment benefits tumbled to their lowest level more than 50 years last week despite a number of economic headwinds including the war in Iran.

entries.title: Trump says he's lifting certain tariffs on Scotch whisky after royal visit
entries.snippet: "The King and Queen got me to do something that nobody else was able to do, without hardly even asking!" President Donald Trump posted on social media.

entries.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, picks Nicole Saphier instead
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said Thursday he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor and radiologist Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

entries.title: U.S. economy grew 2% from January-March, but Iran war clouds outlook
entries.snippet: "This is a split-screen economy."

entries.title: House approves bill to fund Homeland Security and end the record shutdown
entries.snippet: After weeks of delay, the House voted Thursday to fund much of the Department of Homeland Security, but not its immigration enforcement operations, and sent the bipartisan package to President Donald Trump to sign, ending the longest agency shutdown in history.

entries.title: Key inflation gauge jumps to highest level in 3 years as Iran war spikes gas prices
entries.snippet: A key inflation measure jumped in March as gas prices soared, the latest sign that the Iran war is pushing up the cost of living and delaying any interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.

entries.title: Iran's supreme leader vows to protect nuclear and missile capabilities
entries.snippet: Iran's supreme leader vowed Thursday in a defiant tone to protect the Islamic Republic's nuclear and missile capabilities, which U.S. President Donald Trump has sought to curtail through airstrikes and as part of a wider deal to cement the war's shaky ceasefire.

entries.title: Louisiana congressional primaries suspended after Supreme Court ruling, state officials say
entries.snippet: Early voting had been scheduled to begin Saturday in advance of the May 16 primary.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: US: Trump signs DHS funding bill, ending partial shutdown
entries.snippet: The legislation ends a partial government shutdown and restores funding to key government agencies. But Republicans are unhappy that ICE and Border Patrol have been excluded.

entries.title: How Serbia's government dominates the country's media
entries.snippet: Since Serbia's ruling SNS party came to power, it has tightened its grip on the media. With an election expected soon, experts fear that authorities will try to eliminate the last pockets of independent reporting.

entries.title: Saudi Arabia's public fund to withdraw LIV Golf funding
entries.snippet: Public Investment Fund cites "current macro dynamics" as it slashes funding for breakaway golf tour. LIV isn't the only casualty of changing Saudi "investment priorities" amid economic turmoil and war in the Middle East.

entries.title: UK raises terror threat level following antisemitic attack
entries.snippet: The Joint Terrorism Analysis Center raised the terror threat level to severe, which indicates that there's a high likelihood of an attack in the next six months.

entries.title: UAE oil exit from OPEC signals focus on domestic priorities
entries.snippet: Abu Dhabi's exit from OPEC signals a geopolitical shift and a pointed message to Saudi Arabia. What are the ripple effects on ties with Riyadh, the US, and Israel in the short and long term?

entries.title: Iran's Vafaei playing for more than snooker glory
entries.snippet: After years lost to visa battles, Hossein Vafaei is finally delivering on his early promise as a snooker prodigy. He's hoping to inspire people back home amid the turmoil in Iran.

entries.title: Study busts myth: German 'spring fatigue' doesn't exist
entries.snippet: Do we really feel more tired in spring? Researchers in Switzerland say, "there's no such thing as spring fatigue." But it is a deep cultural phenomenon in German-speaking countries.

entries.title: How widespread is global fatigue with the US?
entries.snippet: The global image of the US has taken a blow, as many people are increasingly fed up with the constant stream of controversies surrounding the Trump administration.

entries.title: Belgium seeks nationalization of nuclear power plants
entries.snippet: Belgium is reversing its decadeslong phasing-out course, seeking more energy independence by reviving its nuclear plants.

entries.title: First dots on the roadmap to exiting fossil fuels
entries.snippet: The message from inaugural talks on exiting fossil fuels was clear. It's not if, but when and how.

entries.title: Will EU bring in a windfall tax on oil companies?
entries.snippet: Five countries, including Germany, want the EU to bring in a so-called windfall tax to target energy companies benefiting from the price crisis. It can be done, but it's complex.

entries.title: Iran war: Mojtaba Khamenei says there will be 'change' in Strait of Hormuz 'management'
entries.snippet: Iran's supreme leader issued a statement implying that Tehran wanted to keep its nuclear program and perhaps impose tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. Crude prices remain volatile. DW has more.

entries.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai honored by DW
entries.snippet: Before being imprisoned for 20 years, Jimmy Lai founded Hong Kong's largest pro-democracy newspaper. His son said the award shows that those who fight for the freedom of others "are never alone."

entries.title: Germany news: Economy shows surprise growth despite Iran war
entries.snippet: Germany's economy has grown more than expected, against a backdrop of rising energy costs and uncertain export markets. Meanwhile, VW reports a sharp drop in profits, as pressures mount on the carmaker. DW has more.

entries.title: India news: Exit polls out in key state elections
entries.snippet: Projections suggest mixed outcomes in West Bengal, Kerala, Assam, Puducherry and Tamil Nadu. Narendra Modi's BJP is trying to make inroads in three states where it has never held power.

entries.title: Israel stops Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters
entries.snippet: More than 20 ships were intercepted in a mission aimed at challenging the blockade of the Gaza Strip. Israel dismissed those on board as "attention-seeking agitators."

entries.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
entries.snippet: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

entries.title: Women journalists in Ghana encounter – and overcome – gender barriers
entries.snippet: In Ghana, DW Akademie works with women journalists as they try to advance in their careers despite gender barriers.

entries.title: North Korea faces food shortages as drought threatens crops
entries.snippet: The country's state-run news has said much of the country has been hit by an unusually severe drought. The largely isolated country, with a population of over 26 million, was already facing food shortages.
